rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Frank Bonetti Plumbing, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Peterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate in its moving papers that it propounded contention discovery on plaintiffs and that plaintiffs responses thereto were factually devoid preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The affirmative evidence submitted by defendant, the declarations of Dan and Raymond Bonetti, do not negate an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claim thus defendant failed to sustain its burden under that prong. Even if those declarations are admissible, they merely create a triable issue when read in conjunction with Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
